payload.title: MTA Board Action Items

payload.description: MTA filed a lawsuit to compel federal government to release funding for Phase 2. Preparatory work adjacent to the Metro-North station at 125th Street and Park Avenue is underway to create space for the tunnel boring machine.

payload.description: MTA C&D is undertaking the Project to bring MNR service from the Bronx to Penn Station. Modernizing Amtrak's railroad infrastructure on its Hell Gate Line by realigning tracks, optimizing overhead catenary systems, building electrical power systems, replacing and installing signal equipment, and constructing four new MNR passenger stations.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Project to bring MNR service from the Bronx to Penn Station

payload.description: Pilot program providing enclosed, secure bicycle parking at six suburban commuter rail stations to encourage more customers to bike to and from stations and reduce reliance on cars. Contract with eLock Technologies LLC.

payload.description: Contract for design and engineering services to support development of three Design-Build contracts for accessibility improvements on Queens Boulevard, Grand Concourse and Nostrand Avenue Lines.
Capital Program Committee recommends 13 procurement actions for a proposed expenditure of $1.17 billion.
Bridges and Tunnels procurement agenda - 1 action for $4.3M.
Toll evasion currently costs the MTA more than $300 million per year.
Joint multi-agency task force achieved a 53 percent reduction in ghost plates overall.

payload.raw_value_text: 42 percent reduction in the last year
payload.description: Joint multi-agency task force achieved a 42 percent reduction in ghost plates in the last year.
Largest subway car procurement in MTA history - up to 2,400 cars, through Governor Hochul's $68 billion capital plan.
Governor Hochul's auto insurance reform proposal could save the MTA approximately $50 million annually.

C&D procurement agenda - 3 actions for $25.6M.
Contract award for MTA Crew Dispatch and Management System Implementation, Licensing, and Maintenance for Metro-North Railroad and Long Island Rail Road. GIRO, Inc.

payload.claim_text: A federal court ruled that the federal government cannot terminate the congestion pricing program. Judge Liman issued an additional decision dismissing the Trucking Association's lawsuit. These decisions provide strong affirmation that the congestion pricing program is firmly grounded in established law, is operating effectively, and is here to stay.
payload.subject: Congestion Pricing Program

payload.claim_text: Release of a $600 million FEMA reimbursement related to the MTA's extensive COVID-era cleaning efforts.
payload.subject: FEMA Reimbursement

payload.claim_text: For the first time in MTA history, women now hold a majority of senior leadership positions at Headquarters.
payload.subject: MTA Headquarters senior leadership

payload.description: MTA Board Meeting held from 9:00 - 11:00 AM ET
Regular Board Meeting Minutes, 9:00 AM at 2 Broadway, New York, New York
Board Member Valdivia announced she is resigning from the MTA Board following the April 29, 2026 meeting.
Amendment to license with Web Food Products, Inc. for MTA Police Department vehicle parking at 143-19 94th Avenue, Jamaica, NY - 26 parking spaces, two-year term through January 31, 2028.
Acquisition of property interests along Erskine Place in the Bronx from the City of New York in support of the Penn Station Access Project - sidewalk leading to future Co-op City Station.
License agreement with Dover Greens LLC for commuter parking at MNR Harlem Valley-Wingdale Station - 83 parking spaces, three-year term, $10,000 per annum.
Lease with Beer Table LLC for retail space (Unit A3120, approx. 1,003 sq. ft.) in the Grand Central Madison Concourse - 10 year term for retail sale of craft beer/snacks & groceries.

Board approved award of four competitively solicited personal service contracts for ITS and Operations Systems Consultant Design Services on as-needed basis to Henningson, Durham & Richardson Architecture and Engineering; PBS Engineering; M&J Engineering; and VHB Engineering. Aggregate $15,000,000 over five years.

payload.project_name: Design Services for Three Line Bundles: Queens Boulevard, Grand Concourse and Nostrand Avenue Lines
payload.description: Preliminary design and engineering services to support development of three Design-Build contracts for accessibility improvements and miscellaneous rehabilitation work on NYCT's Queens Boulevard, Grand Concourse and Nostrand Avenue Lines. Each contract includes ADA upgrades, structural repairs, station rehabilitation work. Nostrand Avenue Line also includes escalator replacement.

payload.project_name: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 Community Outreach Services Modification
payload.description: Modification to extend contract with East Harlem Community Collaborators JV for community outreach services in support of Second Avenue Subway Phase 2. Includes staffing and managing SAS Community Information Center in Harlem.

tool_args.label: R211 CBTC Carborne Equipment Software Upgrades

payload.project_name: R211 Carborne CBTC Equipment Software Upgrades
payload.description: Modification to implement several software upgrades to the carborne CBTC equipment for the R211 fleet so it complies with MTA's updated CBTC Interoperability Interface Specification. Issues 4 updates to I2S to improve train speed adjustments, location monitoring, train door and platform monitoring, and prevent unnecessary emergency braking.
Modification to implement software upgrades to carborne CBTC equipment for R211 fleet.

tool_args.label: SAS Phase 2 Community Outreach Modification Value

payload.description: Modification to extend Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 community outreach contract by 14 months to October 31, 2026.
